atDirectives.name: @tailwind
atDirectives.description: Use the `@tailwind` directive to insert Tailwind's `base`, `components`, `utilities` and `screens` styles into your CSS.

atDirectives.name: @apply
atDirectives.description: Use the `@apply` directive to inline any existing utility classes into your own custom CSS.

atDirectives.name: @layer
atDirectives.description: Use the `@layer` directive to tell Tailwind which 'bucket' a set of custom styles belong to. Valid layers are `base`, `components`, and `utilities`.

atDirectives.name: @config
atDirectives.description: Use the `@config` directive to specify which config file Tailwind should use when compiling that CSS file.

atDirectives.name: @plugin
atDirectives.description: Use the `@plugin` directive to load a Tailwind plugin into your CSS file.

atDirectives.name: @theme
atDirectives.description: Use the `@theme` directive to configure your design system tokens.

atDirectives.name: @source
atDirectives.description: Use the `@source` directive to explicitly tell Tailwind where to look for utility classes.

atDirectives.name: @utility
atDirectives.description: Use the `@utility` directive to create custom utilities.
